event: Saint Demetrius as warrior on horseback
symbols: red horse — martial valor and the blood of martyrdom
spear striking downward — victory over evil and the enemies of the faith
gold background — the eternal light of heaven and divine glory
fallen figure beneath the horse — the defeat of persecution and spiritual evil
description: A Byzantine-style Orthodox icon on a gold leaf background depicting a young warrior saint mounted on a rearing red-orange horse. The saint wears ornate dark armor and a red cloak, holding a spear or lance. His name inscription in Greek appears at the upper right. A small fallen figure lies beneath the horse's hooves. The style follows traditional iconographic conventions with flat gold ground, frontal gaze, and decorative patterning on the armor. The icon panel shows age-related wear at the edges.
theological_description: St. Demetrius of Thessaloniki, a Christian soldier martyred under Emperor Maximian around 306 AD, is one of the most venerated military saints in the Orthodox tradition. While a post-biblical figure, the image of the mounted warrior saint defeating evil resonates deeply with biblical themes of spiritual warfare. Ephesians 6:11-12 commands believers to put on the full armor of God to stand against the schemes of the devil. The mounted warrior recalls the rider on the white horse in Revelation 19:11-16 who is called Faithful and True and judges with justice. The victory over a fallen enemy embodies Romans 16:20: 'The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet.' The red horse evokes the apocalyptic horsemen of Revelation 6:4, while the martial imagery connects to the broader biblical theme that the people of God must contend for the faith (Jude 1:3). The gold background declares the eternal, heavenly reality in which the saints dwell after their witness unto death.

scripture_refs.reason: The warrior saint in full armor embodies the command to put on God's armor for spiritual warfare
The mounted warrior in victory echoes the rider called Faithful and True who conquers with righteousness
The enemy crushed beneath the horse's hooves mirrors the promise of Satan crushed underfoot
Contending earnestly for the faith, embodied in the warrior-martyr's defense of Christianity
